naming_note: All names drawn from file 26 expanded name banks, Drenmark/Free Fens register — soft, Celtic-adjacent, worn smooth. No A-name clustering. Calder, Brynn, Wren, Edda, Sova — all Drenmark-appropriate and not previously used. Orveth is a deliberate single-name elder in the Moor Communion tradition.

npcs.name: Orveth
npcs.note: Single name — Moor Communion elder tradition. Family name exists in Station records and is almost never used.
npcs.role: Senior Station-Keeper — Northwest Station, Free Fens
npcs.location: Northwest Station — three days from Mossward into the deep fen
npcs.physical: Very small and very still. The stillness is not frailty — it is economy. Has been moving through fen terrain for eighty-four years and has refined the motion to its minimum necessary expression. White-haired, which in the Fens is not unusual but at this age is specific — the white of someone who has been outside in all weather for eight decades. Her hands show the Communion's work: specific callouses from the physical tools of Station-keeping, marks from decades of handling the medicinal plants that grow only in the northwest fen.
npcs.disposition: The specific patience of someone who has been waiting for something for thirty years and has not been certain it was coming. Is now fairly certain it is coming but is less certain she knows what to do about it than she was when she was waiting.
npcs.religion: Moor Communion — practiced for seventy years, the deepest practitioner in the Fens

schedule.dawn: The dawn practice — the specific Moor Communion morning observance that has not changed in seventy years
schedule.morning: Station-keeping work — the practical maintenance of the northwest Station and its surrounding territory
schedule.midday: With Edda Vrane — the oral tradition recording sessions
schedule.afternoon: The northwest fen — walks she makes alone, following patterns she has followed for thirty years, watching what is changing
schedule.evening: Long sessions of sitting with the Communion's accounting of what the northwest holds. Deciding.

relationships.note: Loves Calder Moss the way old people love the young people who have become what they hoped someone would become. Is deciding whether she has the right to ask what the tradition says may be necessary. Has not asked yet.

Has been sharing oral tradition for six weeks with specific care about what is shared. Is giving Edda the accounting of Gorra's history. Has not yet given her the accounting of what resealing requires.
The Mossward Station-Keeper. Has known Brynn for twenty years. Trusts her judgment. Has been communicating with her about the contamination through Fens message-runners.

what_she_holds.the_gorra_tradition: The complete oral account of Gorra's sealing — what was in the site, what Gorra did, what it cost her, and the specific sequence of the closing. The tradition is not written down anywhere. Edda Vrane is currently recording the historical sections. The sealing sequence has not been shared.
what_she_holds.the_variant: Her constructed interpretation: two people who can see each other clearly enough that neither's perception can be distorted. The shared seeing as a survivable alternative to the solitary seeing. She does not know if this is true. She knows it is possible in the Communion's theoretical framework. She has never had reason to test it.
what_she_holds.what_is_moving_toward: Knows the contamination is moving toward the deep northwest location. Knows what is at that location — something the Communion's oldest accounts describe as a place where the fen's deepest peat holds something that was there before the fen was fen. Not a demonic site. Something older. The movement of the degrading sealing toward this location is not random. Whatever is inside Gorra's site is being drawn to what is in the deep northwest. If it reaches it — Orveth does not finish this thought in her own mind.

deciding_about_asking.the_question: Does she have the right to tell Calder Moss what the tradition says and what she thinks might be possible?
deciding_about_asking.what_she_is_weighing: Calder's life, which she loves. Calder's choice, which she respects. The information Calder needs to make the choice, which she has. The possibility that knowing will create an obligation Calder feels even if not imposed. The alternative — not telling — which leaves the situation to resolve in the worst way.
deciding_about_asking.what_tips_it: A player. If a player arrives at the northwest Station demonstrating the quality of perception that the Moor Communion recognizes — through actions, through how they respond to the contamination, through something Orveth observes rather than something they claim — she will tell Calder about the variant in the player's presence. Making it a shared disclosure rather than a private one changes the weight of what she is asking.

npcs.player_interaction: Accessible at the northwest Station. Difficult to reach — three days from Mossward, off the maintained boardwalk. Players who arrive will find an elderly woman who is unhurried and who asks questions that are more specific than small talk. Sustained presence and demonstrated Fens-appropriate behavior opens the deeper conversation. A player with Moor Communion religious lore above 150 will recognize her as a senior practitioner. A player with the Pale Company quality of attention will receive her specific notice.

npcs.name: Calder Moss
npcs.role: Fisher and hunter — northwest fen, Free Fens. Romanceable.
npcs.location: The northwest fen — rarely in Mossward, occasionally at the northwest Station
npcs.physical: Fen-born in the most literal sense — grew up in a one-room raised house two hours from the nearest boardwalk. The body of someone who has been moving through difficult terrain since childhood: lean, efficient, specific economy of movement that reads as stillness until you watch them navigate bog and realize it is actually constant precise adjustment. Carries a long-handled gaff hook that doubles as a depth-testing pole. The hands of someone who works with nets and lines — the specific callouses of pulling weight against water resistance.
npcs.disposition: Not unfriendly. Economical. Has the quality of someone who has spent significant time alone and has learned to make words count because they cost effort in solitude. Will say three words when three words are sufficient. Will say thirty when thirty are needed.
npcs.financial_state: Self-sufficient — fishing and hunting in the northwest fen, trading surplus at the northwest Station and occasionally at Mossward
npcs.faction: None institutional. Moor Communion practitioner — informal, as Fens-born people practice it without formal affiliation.
npcs.religion: Moor Communion — not practiced through the Station structure but through direct relationship with the fen. Orveth has recognized this as the most genuine form of the Communion she has encountered in thirty years.

schedule.every_three_days: Returns to the northwest contamination zone to take readings — water samples, plant die-off measurements, animal absence radius
schedule.otherwise: Fishing and hunting routes through the northwest fen. Knows every water channel, every stable ground section, every animal run for thirty miles in every direction.

relationships.note: Has known Orveth since childhood. Considers her the person who taught them to read the fen correctly. The weight of what Orveth is carrying is felt but not named — Calder knows something is being decided and waits.

The First Scholars representative is at the northwest Station and asks questions. The questions are good questions. The framework behind them is incomplete in ways that could be dangerous in the northwest fen.

what_they_have_withheld.the_movement: The contamination is moving northwest, not spreading concentrically. Approximately fifty yards per week, measurably consistent. The center of the contamination is further northwest than the See's official map shows.
what_they_have_withheld.the_destination: The movement is heading toward a specific location in the deep northwest fen — a place the animals have always avoided and that their grandmother told them never to approach. Calder has never approached it. Has gone close enough to confirm the animal avoidance is still active and is in fact intensifying.
what_they_have_withheld.why_not_told: If the See sends people to the moving center, those people will be at the wrong location and in the path of the spreading contamination without knowing it. The See's investigators are competent but do not have the northwest fen knowledge to survive a navigation error in the current conditions. Calder does not trust that the See's response, if given incomplete information, will produce a better outcome than waiting until the right understanding is assembled.

the_romance_track.context: The northwest fen is not a social environment. Calder is not a social person. What develops does so in the register of two people who are both in a place that most people would not choose to be, doing necessary work, and gradually recognizing each other's quality.
the_romance_track.early_signals: Calder does not show strangers the northwest fen's routes. The first time they show a player a specific route — without explanation, simply taking the correct path without being asked — is the first signal.
the_romance_track.middle_signals: A question about what the player notices in the fen. Not what they see — what they notice. The distinction matters.
the_romance_track.later_signals: Remaining at the northwest Station through the night rather than returning to their own shelter. The northwest Station is where the sick households are and where the work is.
the_romance_track.the_withholding_thread: A player who earns enough trust will be told what Calder has been withholding — the movement, the destination, the reason for the silence. This is not a test. It is the natural consequence of trust in an environment where trust is the most valuable thing. A player who responds to this information correctly — with the quality of attention that indicates they understand what it means and what it requires — receives something else from Calder that is not verbal.
the_romance_track.the_orveth_connection: Calder's willingness in the resealing attempt — if it comes to that — is not something Orveth can ask for directly. It will emerge from Calder's own decision, made with full information. A player who is present when Orveth makes her disclosure will witness Calder receive information that changes everything. What Calder does with it is their own.

npcs.player_interaction: Found in the northwest fen — not accessible any other way, or only very rarely when they come to Mossward for supplies. The fen_born spawn hook sends a player directly into their territory. Any player who reaches the northwest fen will encounter them within a day. The initial exchange is minimal. The relationship builds through shared work in difficult terrain over multiple encounters.

npcs.name: Brynn Holst
npcs.physical: The practical build of someone who has been doing physical work in difficult conditions for twenty-five years. The Station-Keeper's hands — the same specific callouses as Orveth but less worn, twenty-five years instead of seventy.
npcs.disposition: Organized, methodical, currently sitting with a problem her organization and methodology cannot solve.

the_peat_object.situation: Has been in the locked room of the Mossward Station for three weeks. The identification rite requires identifying the object's nature and approximate origin. She has identified: it is approximately two thousand years old, it is cold to the touch regardless of ambient temperature, it is sealed, and it is made of a material she cannot identify. She cannot proceed to acknowledgment because acknowledgment requires speaking to what the object is and she does not know what it is.
the_peat_object.what_she_has_not_done: Opened it. This is not procedural caution — it is genuine fear. The rite exists because things come up from the peat and some of them require careful handling. The fact that she cannot identify this one is the reason the rite specifies that identification must precede everything else. Opening it without identification is the one thing she will not do alone.
the_peat_object.what_she_needs: Someone with a framework she does not have. Has been considering asking Edda Vrane at the northwest Station — but the First Scholars' framework is textual and the identification rite is practical and sensory. Has been considering asking the See's central authority — but that would require explaining why she has not followed standard procedure for three weeks, which would require explaining that she cannot complete the first step.

relationships.note: Communicates regularly through message-runners. Has been getting Orveth's communications about the northwest contamination and has been increasing the urgency of her messages to the Fen Lords without receiving a resolution.

The First Scholars representative was housed at the Mossward Station before going to the northwest. Has been following Edda's work with specific interest. Considers Edda a potential framework resource for the peat object problem.

npcs.player_interaction: The primary institutional contact in Mossward. The rural midwife spawn hook sends a player directly to her. She is the access point for the northwest Station introduction, the peat object situation, and the Fen Lords authority question through the See's institutional perspective.

npcs.name: Edda Vrane
npcs.role: First Scholars representative — recording Orveth's oral tradition
npcs.location: Northwest Station — has been here six weeks. Stays at the Station rather than returning to Mossward.
npcs.physical: Scholar's build — not soft but not hardened for outdoor work. Has been developing the outdoor hardening over the past six weeks through the specific necessity of being in the northwest fen. The scholar's equipment she arrived with has been supplemented by Fens-practical items that Orveth gave her without comment.
npcs.disposition: Genuinely engaged with the oral tradition and with increasing difficulty maintaining the observer's neutrality that her scholarly training requires. Has been a First Scholars researcher for twelve years and has never been a witness to something active before.

schedule.morning: Recording sessions with Orveth — two hours, careful transcription
schedule.midday: Review and organization of what was recorded, cross-referencing with First Scholars' Archive materials she brought with her
schedule.afternoon: Observation — has been documenting the contamination's visible signs because they are happening and she cannot not document them
schedule.evening: Writing to the Archive of Keth — letters that she writes and does not send because the things she is describing are getting further from anything the letters can adequately convey

npcs.what_she_is_recording: The oral tradition of Gorra's sealing in Orveth's telling — historical context, the nature of the site, what Gorra found, the approach. Orveth has not yet given her the sealing sequence itself.
npcs.what_she_is_witnessing: The contamination spreading from the northwest. Three sick households. Plant die-off visible from the Station. Sound absence. The smell.
npcs.what_she_lacks: The framework to understand what the account and the visible contamination have to do with each other. Has the historical account without the practical implications. Has the visible effects without the theological context. Has been trying to bridge them with pure scholarship and cannot quite reach.
npcs.the_unmatched_letters: Has been writing to the Archive of Keth for four weeks but not sending the letters because each one describes something that has changed since the previous one and the change keeps outpacing the description. Has seven unsent letters in her pack. The seventh, written three days ago, contains the observation that what Orveth is describing sounds exactly like what she is watching happen outside the Station window — but she does not know how to say this in a way that is academically defensible.
npcs.player_interaction: Accessible at the northwest Station. Will speak at length to any player who demonstrates scholarly engagement or genuine interest in understanding what she is witnessing. A player who can provide the framework she lacks — who can explain the demonic taxonomy, the nature of sealed sites, the Pale Company's understanding of such thresholds — will produce a significant change in her. She will send the letters. She will include what the player told her.

npcs.name: Wren Holmswick
npcs.role: Village elder — Fenhollow, leader of the pro-response Fen Lords faction
npcs.location: Primarily Fenhollow. Currently in Mossward pressing the authority question.
npcs.physical: The specific quality of a person whose grief has become practical. Lost a nephew to the early contamination spread eight months ago — he was fishing the northwest channels and encountered the early water contamination. The grief has not softened into acceptance. It has hardened into purpose.
npcs.disposition: Urgent without being irrational. Has the most direct stake in the contamination crisis of any Fen Lord and has become the most informed voice in the Fen Lords through the specific education that proximity to a crisis provides.
npcs.secret: The nephew who died had reported strange water to Wren eight months ago. Wren told him it was probably a seasonal variation — unusual but not unknown. This is the specific guilt underneath the urgency. The urgency is real and justified. Its root is guilt that the urgency is also expiation.
npcs.player_interaction: In Mossward during the authority dispute. Will speak at length about the contamination crisis and the See's need to respond. Her account of the nephew's death is the most humanizing entry point into the crisis for a player who arrives with no prior knowledge.

npcs.name: Caed Daltmere
npcs.role: Village elder — Eastern Fens communities, leader of the pro-caution Fen Lords faction
npcs.location: Eastern Fens primarily. In Mossward for the authority dispute.
npcs.physical: The quality of someone who has been governing a community in difficult conditions for twenty years and has learned to be very careful about which decisions are reversible and which are not.
npcs.disposition: Not wrong about the institutional risk. Specifically wrong about the urgency. Has been operating on the assumption that the northwest contamination is serious but manageable and that the risk of concentrating authority in the See is larger than the risk of the contamination reaching a crisis without centralized response. This assumption has not been tested by anyone who has been to the northwest in the past three weeks.
npcs.secret: Has been in communication with an Ashenveld representative about the situation. Not the census-taker — a separate channel. Has been receiving pressure from Ashenveld to maintain Fens distributed governance, which Ashenveld supports because centralized Fens governance would be harder to eventually subsume. Has not told anyone about this communication. Does not believe his position is wrong because of the Ashenveld communication. May be correct about this.
npcs.player_interaction: His position is the more difficult one to change because it is defensible. A player who can demonstrate the actual state of the northwest contamination — through Calder Moss's unreported information, through direct observation, through Orveth's assessment — will find his position shift. Not his institutional concerns, which are legitimate. His assessment of the urgency, which is wrong.

npcs.name: Sova Fenwick
npcs.role: Hollow Road guide and contact — Free Fens routes
npcs.location: Various — operates from a series of maintained shelters throughout the Fens
npcs.physical: Seven years of Fens navigation in how she moves. The specific quality of someone who knows exactly where they are at all times and has learned to project calm about this because panic in the Fens is expensive.
npcs.disposition: Professional, direct, specifically Fens-adapted in her moral framework — operates on the Hollow Road's professional neutrality but with the Fens community's sense of obligation to the people who live here.
npcs.financial_state: Hollow Road income — comfortable by Fens standards
npcs.religion: Moor Communion — practical observation without formal affiliation

the_moved_person.situation: Three weeks ago was asked through the Hollow Road network to move a specific person out of the northwest quickly. The request came from outside the Fens — from the broader Hollow Road, not a local contact. She moved the person from the northwest Station to a staging point on the Fens' western edge. The person continued west.
the_moved_person.who_the_person_was: Did not ask. Standard Hollow Road practice. The person was young — mid-twenties, Fens-born by their movement, scared in a specific way that suggested they had seen something rather than been threatened.
the_moved_person.why_she_is_thinking_about_it: Has not heard from the staging point that the person arrived safely, which they should have three weeks ago. Has sent two messages asking for confirmation. Has received no reply.
the_moved_person.what_she_suspects: The person was one of the early contamination-exposure cases. The sickness presented slowly enough that they appeared functional when they left and the symptoms advanced on the road.
the_moved_person.player_interaction: The hollow road runner spawn hook entry point. Her information about the moved person connects the Hollow Road network to the contamination crisis. A player who can trace what happened to the person — through medical contacts, through the western staging point — will find information about the contamination's early-stage presentation that changes the medical picture.

npcs.name: Drev Coldwater
npcs.role: Fen farmer — northwest fen community, Orveth's neighbor
npcs.location: A farmstead two hours south of the northwest Station
npcs.physical: Seventy years of farming fen ground in the most specific possible. Does not look frail because he is not frail. Looks like the fen looks — present, enduring, not in any particular hurry.
npcs.disposition: Does not waste words. At seventy in the northwest fen, has concluded that words are less informative than what you do with your hands.
npcs.financial_state: Self-sufficient — needs nothing from the outside
npcs.what_he_knows: Has been watching the northwest contamination from his farmstead's southern boundary for eighteen months — longer than anyone else has been actively documenting it. Has not told anyone because nobody asked and he does not volunteer. His observation records are in his memory and in a series of notched sticks that he uses to track environmental changes and that constitute an eighteen-month detailed record of the contamination's spread.
npcs.the_notched_sticks: Each stick represents a ten-day period. The notch pattern records: water smell intensity (on a scale from 0 to 5 that he established himself), animal presence at specific markers he has maintained for forty years, plant health at three observation points, and one additional marker he added eighteen months ago: a specific quality of the morning light from the northwest that he noticed first and has been recording ever since. The First Scholars' Edda Vrane does not know these records exist. Orveth does not know they are this detailed.
npcs.player_interaction: The hedge farmer spawn hook entry point. His teaching is available to someone who works the fen correctly. His records are available to someone he trusts. His trust requires demonstrated competence at fen farming specifically — not general farming, fen farming, which is different enough that a general farmer who does not learn quickly will fail the test within a day.
